domain: Yiddish badkhn (Ashkenazic wedding bard improvising rhyming verse on the spot for specific persons; quality evaluated by audience for both creativity and accuracy; jointly required)
specific_mechanism: Person-specific live-improvised verse evaluated jointly for creativity and accuracy by audience; quality requires BOTH dimensions simultaneously.
llm_application: BADKHN-EVAL: a dual-axis evaluation diagnostic. For each test prompt + ground-truth target persona facts F, the evaluator computes (a) c = semantic novelty of generated output vs base-corpus (creativity); (b) f = factual accuracy against F (accuracy). Final score = sqrt(c × f) — geometric mean. Geometric mean PENALISES either-axis collapse: high c × low f = ~0 (creative hallucination); low c × high f = ~0 (boring copy). Both required. Differs from BLEU/ROUGE (single-axis surface match) and FactScore (single-axis fact-check) by JOINT geometric mean.
novelty_claim: BLEU/ROUGE/BERTScore are single-axis surface similarity. FactScore is single-axis factual. G-Eval is single-axis LLM-as-judge. Self-BLEU measures diversity alone. BADKHN-EVAL differs by (a) DUAL-AXIS creativity+accuracy jointly, (b) GEOMETRIC MEAN penalising collapse, (c) PERSON-SPECIFIC target facts as anchor. Joint formulation novel.
